mentals.text: MIND. ► He feels light-spirited; the intellectual and physical powers appear to be excited (first day), [3] . ► (He feels excessively contented), [1] . ► He takes everything in the worse sense, and weeps and howls for hours (second day), [1] . ► The boy weeps constantly against his will , for half an hour, [1] . ► Depressed as with grief, early in the morning on waking ; consciousness is not clear, [1] . ► Depressed and friendless; he wishes only to be left alone, in the forenoon (the eighth day), [3] . ► The person images only disagreeable, sad images (first day), [5] . ► Involuntary sighings and groanings, as in great pain; he is not conscious of it, [6] . ► He believes he will not be able to recover his health, [4] . [10.] ► He feels low-spirited on account of his disease, [1] . ► *She is constantly possessed by bad thoughts, which oblige her to weep; at the same time she feels apprehensive and uneasy, as if something evil were to happen to her; everything that she only looks at, fills her with sadness (eleventh day), [3] . ► Upon seeing blood, or knives, horrible thoughts throng her mind; she feels, for instance, as though she would commit suicide, although she has the greatest aversion to it, [1] . ► *Intolerable ennui, an hour seems to him half a day , [6] . ► Serious, anxious mood , [1] . ► Anxious, reflective, peevish mood, [5] . ► Oppressive anxiety, attended with emptiness and confusion of the head, and pressure in the forehead (after twelve hours), [2] . ► Anxiety, with external heat and uneasiness as if she had done something bad , [1] . ► *Anxiety, early in the morning, as though he were threatened with an epileptic fit, [1] . ► Anxiety and fearfulness, as if he had committed a crime (fifth day), [5] . [20.] ► Anguish, with much uneasiness, the whole day (second day), [3] . ► Anxiety, with palpitation of the heart, and pulsations in some parts of the breast and abdomen (fourth day), [5] . ► Uneasiness , in the evening, as though some evil were impending , [1] . ► Extremely frightened, and starts upon hearing the least thing fall, [1] . ► *He is apprehensive of losing his thoughts, and his understanding, [1] . ► Dissatisfied with everything, feels despairing, [1] . ► Ill-humored and peevish; she is grumbling continually, [4] . ► Not disposed for anything; nothing gives him pleasure, [3] . ► Peevish, ill-humored; she is conscious of it, at one o'clock in the afternoon (first day), [3] . ► *Peevish and whining ; the lobules of the ears are hot (after two days), [1] . [30.] ► Extremely peevish and obstinate , [1] . ► Is opposed to the wishes of other people, [1] . ► She is excessively peevish, and everything is offensive to her; she desires nothing but to quarrel and make a fuss, in the afternoon (fifth day), [3] . ► The person is greatly excited, overfatigued, and nevertheless discontented as if not enough had been done, [1] . ► He sneers at everybody contemptuously, [1] . ► Indifference, absence of mind, and peevishness, [5] . ► *His mood changes greatly , [1] . ► *Frequently changing mood during the day, sometimes assurance, sometimes timidity , [1] . ► Increased animation alternating with absence of mind, during which one's thoughts, sight, and hearing, are indistinct, and have almost disappeared, [1] . ► *He constantly speaks wrong, choosing different expressions from those which he intended, [1] . [40.] ► Want of attention in reading; the mind does not remain fixed on one object (first day), [5] . ► Incapacity for connected thought, [1] . ► Dulness of mind, [1] . ► Inability and want of disposition for mental labor, [6] . ► Great absence of mind and irresolution (second day), [5] . ► Reluctance to any kind of employment (first day), [5] . ► Want of disposition for any kind of labor, with ennui, in the forenoon , [1] . ► The mind is occupied with a variety of objects, but not one of them leaves a distinct recollection in the mind (fourth day), [5] . ► *Absence of all power of recollection, and great weakness of memory, [1] . ► Continual great weakness of memory, [1] . [50.] ► Want of memory for many weeks, [6] . ► Great forgetfulness, [5] . ► Striking forgetfulness, [6] . ► Great stupor, *with dread of falling forward , [1] .

head.text: HEAD. ► Confusion of the head, with heat in the face, [5] . ► Confusion of the head, with dread of losing his consciousness, [1] . ► Early in the morning, the head feels confused and dull; this passed off after rising (third day), [3] . ► The head feels extremely confused, as if his consciousness were outside of his body; when he says anything, it seems to him as though somebody else had said it; or when he sees anything, it seems as though somebody else had seen it, or as if he saw it through somebody else's eyes, [1] . ► Early in the morning his head feels cloudy and hot, [1] . ► Her head feels befogged and intoxicated; it seems to her as though she would turn about, for nine days, this alternated with pain in the kidneys, and the cloudiness of the head decreased in proportion as the pain increased, and vice versâ (after thirty days), [4] . [60.] ► Great heaviness of the head, with paleness of face, and lassitude, [1] . ► Heaviness of the head, attended with confusion of the forehead, and sensitiveness of the vertex to the touch, at four o'clock in the afternoon (second day), [3] . ► Heaviness of the head when sitting erect, he feels as if he would fall forward, this increases on stooping, at 1 P.M. (fifth day), [3] . ► Early in the morning the head feels heavy and hot, [1] . ► Occasional weakness of the head, [1] . ► Vertigo in the morning, [1] . ► Quickly passing vertigo in the morning , [4] . ► Vertigo increased on stooping, [4] . ► *Vertigo, everything turns with him in a circle , attended with nausea, [1] . ► Everything before his eyes turns with him, [1] . [70.] ► Vertigo even to falling; the whole room seems to turn with her ; she is obliged to sit down at once, at four o'clock in the afternoon (third day), [4] . ► Severe vertigo in walking or sitting, as though he would fall over; often for several days, and with stiffness in the nape of the neck, extending towards the head, [1] . ► Vertigo, even to falling, during a walk; she had to lean against a wall (twenty-fourth day), [4] . ► *Vertigo, early in the morning, as though she would turn around, with a fainting nausea ; after having breakfasted on wheat bread, her nausea ceased, but a sour taste remained in the mouth (eleventh day), [4] . ► Giddiness for some days almost without interruption; it is like slight intoxication produced by beer (thirty-first day), [4] . ► Giddiness the whole day, even to falling; in order to moderate this vertigo, she is obliged to wipe her eyes (eleventh day), [4] . ► *The head always feels giddy as soon as she opens her eyes (sixteenth day), [4] . ► On stooping, surging in the head, synchronous with the pulse; on becoming erect, it is somewhat diminished (after dinner), [3] . ► Over the head, externally a sensation as though the skin would go to sleep (three days), [6] . ► Violent headache, peculiar, as though the brain were dashed to pieces, [6] . [80.] ► Headache, increasing by walking in the open air, [1] . ► Headache, when in bed at night, [1] . ► Pain in the head and nape of the neck; it increases on going to bed, and only leaves off in the morning, on rising, [1] . ► Headache, which obliges one to lie down; with dry heat and cough during sleep, the whole afternoon, [1] . ► Heat rises into his head, from the stomach, in the forenoon, [3] . ► External pinching and griping on the head, with a sensation as of freezing towards the occiput, worse on stooping, in the evening, [4] . ► After dinner, heat in the head; on stooping, a sensation in the brain as if it would fall forwards; this passes off on raising the head (first day), [3] . ► Pain as if the head were being screwed together, with stitches in the forehead, and such a violent heaviness in the vertex, that the head threatens to fall down on stooping; when standing, at 2 o'clock in the afternoon (two days), [3] . ► Dull, pressive headache, increased on walking (first day), [5] . ► Screwing pressure in the head, [1] . [90.] ► Tearing in the whole head, in the forenoon (first day), [3] . ► Headache, tearing, with a sense as of pressing from both sides, in the evening (after two and a half hours), with chills, many evenings in succession, [1] . ► Thrusts, as with a knife, now and then shooting through the head, [1] . ► Stitches in some parts of the head, [3] , [5] . ► Headache, violent stitches in the brain , with inclination to vomit, [1] . ► Stitches in the head from within outwards, in the afternoon (eighth day), [3] . ► Stitches shooting through the head at every step, [1] . ► A stitch in the head, going round the brain, [1] . ► Sharp stitches in the right side of the head, when stooping during labor (twenty-eighth day), [3] . ► Stitches in the fore and back part of the head after eating, worse towards evening (after thirty-seven days), [14] . [100.] ► Pulsative headache on ascending the stairs, after dinner (second day), [5] . ► Rhythmical beating in the whole head, in the afternoon, when walking, and next morning in bed (after three days), [3] . ► Headache, as if bruised, with redness of cheeks, [6] . ► Headache, as if a worm were crawling under the skull; the pain is also cutting, and corrosive, [1] . ► Intolerable itching of the head; he is obliged to scratch until the parts bleed; after scratching, the skin pains, [2] . ► Itching, formication here and there over the head, [3] . ► Sense of warmth in the forehead, which is not perceptible externally; with dulness of the mind, for half an hour, [3] . ► Burning and pressive pain, with warmth in the forehead , after dinner, when standing or sitting ; the pain is relieved in the open air, and does not return in the room, [3] . ► Sense of constriction around the forehead, attended with aching (after a quarter of an hour), [3] . ► Compressive (pinching) headache in the forehead, above the eyes; it seems to come from the temples (after three to twelve hours), [1] . [110.] ► Compressive headache above the eyes, coming from both temples, in the evening; and at night, heat when in bed, followed by sweat (after two hours), [1] . ► Pressing in pain in the forehead, [3] . ► Intensely pressive headache above the eyes, [1] . ► Pressing out at the forehead, after dinner, [3] . ► Tearing in the forehead, in the forenoon; in the afternoon, it is changed to stitching (second day), [3] . ► Tearing headache in the forehead, which is relieved in the open air, in the evening (third day), [3] . ► 10.30 P.M. with the frontal headache, tearing, pressing from the forehead back over the vertex (first day). ► Stitches in the forehead, with dulness and heaviness of the head, in the afternoon (third day), [3] . ► Stitches in the forehead on going to sleep (tenth day), [4] . ► Beating and stitches in the forehead and right side of the head, in the afternoon (second day), [3] . [120.] ► Sensation as if something were crawling (between the skin and flesh), coming from both temples, and going to the forehead, here it presses outward as though it would penetrate through, [6] . ► At the upper part of the forehead, a little spot is painful to the touch, early in the morning (tenth and eleventh days), [5] . ► Confusing tension in a small spot in the right temple; it passes off by pressure; when the pressure ceases, it returns immediately, in the forenoon (two days), [3] . ► Continual boring and tearing in both temples, in the forenoon (fourth day), [3] . ► Painful boring into the right temple, in the evening (fifth day), [3] . ► Boring and drawing pain in the region of the left temple, in the evening (third day), [5] . ► Pressing-in pain in the right temple, after dinner, [3] . ► External tearing in the right temple, followed by continual internal boring and beating (second day), [3] . ► Tearing in the right temple; rubbing excites a burning in the place (after half an hour to two hours), [3] . ► Tearing upwards in the left temple, and subsequent stitches in the right (after two hours), [3] . [130.] ► A stitch in the right temple, as with a large, blunt instrument, leaving transient soreness behind (after thirteen days), [4] . ► Stitches in the temples on singing; they abated as soon as she stopped singing (after thirty-three days), [4] . ► Pain in the left region of the vertex; as if somebody were raising her up by a lock of hair (first day), [1] . ► Beating and raging in the vertex , in the forenoon (two days), [3] . ► Stitches in the head towards the vertex (second and third days), [2] . ► Pulsative headache in the vertex , over the right temple, early on waking, [1] . ► Beating and pressing in the right temple, like strong pulsation, with pressure upon the vertex, as from a heavy weight, in the afternoon (second day), [3] . ► Stitches on the outside, as with an awl, in a small spot of the left side of the head, close to the vertex, [4] . ► Beating and tearing in the upper part of the right side of the head, 1 P.M., [3] . ► Headache, painful drawing in the right side of the head, [1] . [140.] ► Sensation as if the right side of the head were pressed or screwed towards the other, and as if the vertex were oppressed with a heavy load, in the forenoon, at 8 1/2 o'clock (second day), [3] . ► Tearing and stitches in the right side of the head, in the forenoon; and in the left frontal eminence, in the evening (first day), [3] . ► Headache on the left side (after eighteen hours), [2] . ► Short, lasting, dull pain in the occiput (after half an hour), [5] . ► Headache in the occiput, it feels bruised; passes off on lying down, [1] . ► Pressure on the occiput and forehead externally, as from a tight hat , [1] . ► Drawing and pulsative tension in the right side of the occiput, in the forenoon (one day), [3] . ► Dryness of the hairs, [1] . ► Falling of the hair (after eight days), [4] . ► The hairy scalp itches, and is covered with white scales, [2] . [150.] ► *On touching the hair on the head, pain as if the place were sore, [1] .

Stitching, burning pain in head, with vertigo, worse in morning, but relieved by food. Pressure in forehead as from a tight hat. Inability to walk except with eyes open. Throbbing headache, with constipation. Vertigo, with nausea; better after breakfast. Falling out of hair; scalp itches and is numb

Dizziness, whirling sensation, everything turns in a circle, most frequently so as to cause falling; sometimes with nausea, or tension in the nape of the neck; before breakfast; on opening eyes; when closing eyes. Easily made drunk. Vertigo, with white stars before the eyes. State of intoxication after smoking tobacco, or after having taken the weakest spirituous drink, and principally in the morning. Headache, as if one were dragged by the hair; or smart shootings in the brain, with inclination to vomit. Stitches in the brain with nausea. Heaviness of the head, with paleness of face, and fatigue. Compressive cephalalgia. Headache with constipation. Headache from chronic catarrhs in the head. Beatings and pulsations in the head. Congestion of blood towards the eyes and nose, with pressure in the forehead and epistaxis, with a chill when walking in the open air; better after lying or eating. Headache while walking in the open air; > lying down, the head being softly supported. Itching in the forehead. Pain, as from excoriation, in the scalp. (Moist crusts on the temples.). Dryness of the hair. Itching in the scalp, with profuse desquamation.

Headache attended with nausea, pressure in forehead and congestion to eyes and nose, with nosebleed. Pressure in forehead from without inward, or rather outward, or over eyes ; in evening, sometimes with a chill, or nocturnal heat or sweat. Throbbing, frontal pain, < going up-stairs, or stepping. Burning, pressive pain, with heat in forehead, while standing or sitting, > in open air. Headache sometimes in left side, < by walking in open air. Lacerating pain in head. Severe stitches in brain, with nausea and dulness of mind. Sensation in head as if its contents were in a vise, with a weight on top. Headache : from chronic catarrhs of head ; with constipation ; > by lying quiet in bed, or resting head upon a cushion. Heaviness of head, with pale, languid face ; vertex painful to touch. Pulsations in vertex with congestion to eyes and nose. Stupefying tightness in right temple, > by pressing upon it. Heat in head. Headache in region of both parietal bones. θ Spinal disease. Pulsative headache in vertex, over right temple, early on waking. Pain in vertex, < on moving head or stooping, > by pressure ; shooting in vertex on coughing. Tightness, with drawing and beating in right side of occiput. Pain in head and nape of neck, it increases on going to bed, and only leaves off in morning on rising. Semilateral affections of head, old rheumatic affections, always appearing on same side. Increased pulsation of temporal arteries. θ Spinal disease. Itching of scalp, with dry, white scales. Humid scurf, < about temples, bleeding when scratched ; < in evening, or at new and full moon. Scalp feels numb. Pressure on forehead, as from a tight hat. Headache, as if hair was pulled ; with nausea. Falling off and excessive dryness of hair ; scalp sore when hair is touched ; creeping and titillation of scalp. Pain in head and nape of neck < on going to bed ; leaves on rising in morning. Itching on forehead. Exanthema on forehead, feels as if glue had dried on it.

eyes.text: EYES. ► Redness of the eyes, with a sense of excoriation in the corners, and weakness of sight; in the evenings, when reading, he sees a halo around the light; he is obliged to wipe his eyes often; in the right, they become agglutinated; this lasts a long time, [2] . ► Redness of the right eye, with soreness and lachrymation (third and fourth days), [2] . ► Squinting of both eyes , [5] . ► Trembling of the left eye, as though it would leap out; worse towards evening, and on looking down; better on closing the eye, or on looking up, or on holding the eye with the hand; at the same time the eye is so sensitive to the light that she was often obliged to close it for three days after (forty-seven days), [4] . ► Burning sensation of the eyes, morning, on waking, [3] . ► Burning in the eyes, especially on looking up, [1] . ► *Burning and pressure in the eyes , [1] . ► Burning and pressure of the eyes and nose, as if she would be attacked with a cold, [4] . ► Tearing in the upper border of the orbit, [1] . [160.] ► Tension around the left eye (two days), [3] . ► Pressure in the eyes ; she was unable to open them, [1] . ► *Pressure in the eyes, and sensitiveness to the light, [2] . ► Pressure in the right eye, in the evening, when writing or reading, [5] . ► Pressure in the left eye, as if something had fallen into it, just below the upper eyelid; the whole day (fifth day), [4] . ► Biting in the left eye, as from soap, in the evening, [1] . ► Tearing-biting pain in the eye, [1] . ► Biting and burning pain in the eyes, early in the morning (second day), [5] . ► Sensation of excoriation in the interior of the eyes, in the evening; after this the eyes were irresistibly closed, [1] . ► Frequent and violent itching of the eyes, [1] . [170.] ► Sensation of trembling of the eyebrows, [5] . ► The eyelashes fall out easily, [1] . ► On the left lower eyelid, a pimple, with a stinging pain, [1] . ► Incipient sty, frequently forming on the upper eyelid, [2] . ► On closing the eyes but lightly, they are often spasmodically closed with pain; if he tries to open his eyes in the night, they pain him as if they were oppressed with a sudden flood of bright sunlight, even in darkness; they become again drawn together; these symptoms are accompanied by little sleep, and by frequent twitching during the day in the right upper eyelid, [2] . ► Weakness of the eyelids; they are all the time on the point of closing, although he is not sleepy (in the afternoon), [3] . ► He cannot well open the left eye, because it seems to him as if the upper eyelid were handing deep down; on this account he often wipes the eye, in order to see better (fifth day), [3] . ► *The upper eyelid feels paralyzed, hangs down, and covers only half of the eye (twenty-ninth day), [1] . ► Biting pain, with burning and stinging, as from an acrid humor in one of the corners of the eye, [1] . ► Every evening, burning and dryness of the eyelids, with pain in the left internal canthus, and a secretion of dry gum, every morning, lasting upwards of a week, [2] . [180.] ► Stitches in the lower eyelids, [2] . ► Tearing in the right upper eyelid on looking down; on looking up, feeling as if the upper lid were longer and were hanging down; afterwards, stitches in the right side of the head, early in the morning (two days), [3] . ► Sensation of excoriation and dryness in the internal canthus, [2] . ► Alternating pressure in the eyelids (one day), [5] . ► *Itching in the corners of the eyes , and of the lids, [3] . ► Stitches in the corners of the eye, [3] . ► Burning in the corners of the eyes (two to three days), [3] . ► The right eye constantly secretes a mucous humor (eye-gum), [1] . ► The eyes secrete much mucus in the night, for several days in succession, [4] . ► Much dry gum early in the morning on waking, [1] . [190.] ► *The eyes are agglutinated on waking in the morning; they burn on being opened, with dread of light , [2] , [3] . ► *Nightly agglutination for several weeks, with inflammation of the conjunctiva and secretion of mucus during the day; in the evening, when the room is lighted, it seems as if gauze were before the eyes, which is not removed by wiping; he sees a halo around the light (after ten days), [2] . ► The eyes are agglutinated in the morning; dim, with biting (second day), [3] . ► Lachrymation in the open air, [1] . ► Frequent lachrymation , without pain, [3] , [4] . ► Lachrymation early in the morning, after waking, [2] , [3] . ► Lachrymation and burning of the eyes, with a sensation of swelling of the face (first day), [3] . ► Burning and increased secretion of mucus of the eyes, at night, and sometimes early in the morning, with itching, [5] . ► Inflammation of the conjunctiva of the right eye, without much pain, in the evening (first day), [5] . ► Pressure upon the left eyeball (after half an hour), [5] . [200.] ► The eyes become weak after long looking (fourth day), [3] . ► Twitching and mist before the eyes, [5] . ► Dim-sightedness, as through a fog , [1] . ► Dim-sightedness, sometimes in the open air, and passing off in the room, [3] , [5] . ► Dim-sightedness, as through mist, in the evening (one to thirty days), [3] , [5] . ► *Dim-sightedness, which obliges her to wipe her eyes constantly, which relieves, with a sensation as if the lids would adhere in the corners (eleventh day), [3] , [5] . ► The right eye is dim, as if a feather or a hair were before it, which she thinks she must take away (sixth and seventh days), [4] . ► *In the evening, her eyes are so dull and dry that she can neither read nor sew; also during the day her sight is dull (usually her sight is very good), [1] . ► Short flickering and a kind of spots before the eyes, with a sort of vertigo, [1] . ► On closing his eyes, it is bright before them, [1] . [210.] ► The objects she looks at appear yellow (thirty-fourth to thirty-fifth day), [3] . ► After blowing the nose, white little stars sparkle before the eyes (fourth day), [3] .
Objects look yellow. Eyes feel cold. Lids dry, burn, smart, thickened, aggravated in morning; chronic conjunctivitis. Ptosis. Strabismus

Pressure on the eyes, which renders it impossible to open them. Pressure, as from a grain of sand, in the corner of the eye, in the evening. Sensation of burning in the eyes, with nocturnal agglutination of the lids, and diurnal lachrymation. Sensation of coldness in the eyes and eyelids on walking in open air. Swelling of the eyelids. Paralysis of the upper lid. Hordeolum. Eyelashes fall out. Inclination to stare. Spasmodic drawing of the lids at night, with pain in the eyes on opening them. Stitches in the eyes. Photophobia. Confusion of sight, as from a mist, and sparkling before the eyes. Yellow aspect of all objects. Coloured reflection round the candle in the evening. Strabismus of both eyes; esp. loss of power of internal rectus. Glittering before the eyes on shutting them. Far sight.

Far-sightedness. Vision dim and eyes dry in evening. θ Chronic catarrh. Seeing fiery spots ; met. θ Spinal disease. White stars before eyes, with vertigo. Dim-sightedness, like looking through a fog, or as if feathers were before eyes ; must rub eyes. Objects appear yellow. θ Liver complaint. Burning and pressure in eyes. Red eyes with lachrymation. Eyes inflamed, itching at inner canthus ; agglutination at night, and lachrymation by day ; yellow halo around candle ; burning and dryness, smarting, but with very little ulceration. Sensation of coldness in eyes when walking in open air. Spasmodic closure of lids at night, and burning in eyes in morning and evening. θ Ophthalmia. Inclination to stare. Strabismus of either eye ; especially from loss of power of internal rectus. Pressure in eyes, which are sensitive to light. Stitches in eyes. In evening pressure in canthus as from a grain of sand. Eyelids thickened, dry, burning. Chronic granular lids. Eyelids stick together with matter ; lachrymation. Upper lids seem to hang down as if paralyzed, especially left. θ Old, dry granular lids. Burning and dryness in lids every evening, with pain in internal canthus of left eye, with much dry mucus in morning on waking. Burning, dryness ; burning on waking, especially on looking up, with dread of light. Eyes generally better from being bathed. Itching in canthi with burning. Eyelashes fall out. Tottering and falling when closing eyes ; met. θ Spinal disease.

ears.text: EARS. ► On the right ear, a transparent vesicle, without pain, [6] . ► It seems to her as if something were lying before her ear externally, [1] . ► Itching burning in the anterior border of the right ear (first day), [3] . ► Heat and redness of the ear for many evenings , [1] . ► Tension in the ears (second and third days), [5] . ► Boring pain in the ear in the morning, and in the hollow of the ear in the afternoon; also felt on pressure (fourth day), [3] . ► (Headache), tearing and stitches in the ears, somewhat lessened by pressure with the hand, lasting four days, towards evening (after sixth day), [4] . ► Tearing in, behind and below the ears, [3] . [220.] ► Frequent stabs, as with a knife, into the hollow of the hear, [3] . ► Stickings into the ears, [3] . ► Stitches in the ears , especially in the evening (after thirty days), [2] . ► In the night, stitches deep in the right ear (after four hours), [3] . ► Stitches through the ears from within outwards (after four hours), [5] . ► Stitches in the left ear (seventh day), [4] . ► Pulsations in the ear, [1] . ► Severe itching in both ears; rubbing with the fingers increases it (after fifty hours), [1] . ► Itching before and behind the ears and of the lobules, [3] . ► Discharge of pus from the right ear often (eleven days), [2] . [230.] ► On blowing her nose something obstructed her ear; on swallowing, the ear opens again, [1] . ► Itching and tingling in the external meatus, [3] , [5] . ► Crackling of the tympanum, especially when chewing, [1] . ► On swallowing, there is a crackling in the ear, [1] . ► For an hour it seems, in the right ear, as though he had an entirely different voice (fourth day), [3] . ► Loud whistling in the ear, [1] . ► Whizzing in the ears, [1] . ► Roaring of the ears early in the morning; at the same time the stools are more solid than usual, [4] . ► Roaring before the ears, as of large bells, early in the morning, after rising, [1] . ► Roaring in the ears, in the evening, [1] .
Humming; roaring. Eustachian tube feels plugged

Shooting pains in the ears, principally in the evening, or at night. Stitches in the (l.) ear (evening). Itching and sensation of burning in the ears. Frequently, in the evening, heat and redness of one ear. Purulent discharge from the ears. Crackling noise and buzzing in the ears, chiefly when chewing, but also on swallowing.

Buzzing as if outside ear. Humming ; roaring ; whistling ; sound as of large bells. Redness and heat of one ear ; evenings. Hot earlobes with peevishness and whining. Sensation as if something lay before ear ; on blowing nose it is felt, swallowing removes it. Stitches in ears, evening or night. Purulent otorrhœa. Crepitation in ears on swallowing or chewing.

nose.text: NOSE. [240.] ► *The septum of the nose is swollen, red, and painful to the touch ; in the evening the pains are increased, with stitches in the forehead (one day), [4] . ► *Swelling and hardness of the left ala of the nose , with pain to the touch (eight days), [4] . ► *Ulcerated nostrils , [1] . ► A boil on the nose, [1] . ► Rattling and piping in the nose, with rough voice, in the afternoon (three days), [3] . ► The nose is stopped (one day), [1] . ► Obstruction of the left nostril (ten days), [5] . ► Unwell, as from a cold which cannot develop, [1] . ► Sensation in the nose as if a cold would come on, in the evening, for several days (four days), [5] . ► Catarrh, with sneezing and obstruction of the nose, the whole day (three days), [3] . [250.] ► Dry catarrh (nine days), [3] . ► Violent dry catarrh, especially at night, with great dryness of the mouth, [1] . ► Violent fluent coryza in the left nostril, appearing suddenly; the right nostril is obstructed entirely, [1] . ► First fluent coryza, then dry, so that both nostrils are entirely obstructed, [1] . ► On the right side of the nose there are two pimples, with a burning and stinging pain, [1] . ► Frequent sneezing (and hiccough), without coryza (one, two, seven days), [3] . ► Water runs from the right nostril, without catarrh, [3] . ► Secretion of much thick and viscid mucus from the nose, [2] . ► Fluent coryza (with rough voice) in the afternoon and morning (four, six days), [3] . ► Fluent coryza, with frequent sneezing and lachrymation, [4] . [260.] ► Bleeding from the nose, [1] . ► Blood only is blown out, [3] . ► Intermittent burning pain in the right wing of the nose in the evening (one day), [5] . ► Tearing in and near the right nasal fossa; upon pressing the part, it passes off only for a short while (second day), [3] . ► *Soreness with scabs in the right nostril, with a discharge of much thick yellowish mucus (the first four weeks), [3] . ► Violent itching of one ala of the nose (one hour), [1] . ► Itching of the dorsum, the side, and around the orifice of the nose, [3] . ► Excessively acute smell, [1] . ► Weakness of smell, [1] . ► Sour smell in the nose, early in the morning (three days), [3] . >>>>> Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Alum.

Pain at root of nose. Sense of smell diminished. Fluent coryza. Point of nose cracked, nostrils sore, red; worse touch. Scabs with thick yellow mucus. Tettery redness. Ozœna atrophica sicca. Membranes distended and boggy

Pain in the root of the nose and in the forehead. Pain, swelling, and redness of the nose. Soreness and scabs in the nose, with discharge of thick yellow mucus. Discharge of solid, yellow, greenish substances from the nose. Accumulation and flow of a thick and yellowish matter from the nose. Nostrils ulcerated. Furunculus in the nose. Ozæna. Blood from the nose when blown. Epistaxis. Sour smell in the nose. Smell either exceedingly delicate or weak. Coryza, with defluxion from one nostril, with stoppage of the other. Coryza, alternately dry and flowing. Stoppage of the nose.

Sense of smell weak. Nosebleed with pressure in forehead and congestion to eyes. Disposition to colds in head. Nose stopped up, more left side, with glairy mucus. Fluent coryza, with frequent sneezing, free from one nostril, the other obstructed ; lachrymation. Chronic nasal catarrh, with scurfy sore nostrils, and discharge of thick yellow mucus. Catarrhs of long standing with old people. Discharge of dry, hard, yellow-green mucus from nose ; nose swollen, red and sore to touch, worse in evening. Nose dry ; met. θ Spinal disease. Violent pain in root of nose. θ Ozæna. Copious, yellow, sour-smelling mucus, with sore nostrils. Septum narium swollen, red and painful to touch ; more painful evenings. Redness of nose. Nose ulcerated. Swelling and hardness of left alæ nasi. Point of nose cracked. After blowing nose, glittering before eyes.

mouth.text: MOUTH. [310.] ► Thick, badly smelling mucus on the teeth (fifth day), [3] . ► Two rotten molar teeth (one above, the other below) catch into each other on opening the mouth, [6] . ► Ulceration of the roots of all the teeth, [6] . ► In chewing the least she suffers the most violent toothache; the roots of the teeth are then painful, as if they were ulcerated, [1] . ► The toothache extends down to the larynx, with irritation of the nerves, as after having caught cold, or having used too much chamomile, [6] . ► The teeth are very painful when she chews; she is afraid of pressing them against one another (after second day), [1] . ► Upon setting the teeth together, a toothache, as if the teeth were loose, [1] . ► The hollow teeth are very painful when food gets into them, [1] . ► Sense of coldness of the teeth, with great sensitiveness, [3] . ► Sensation as if the teeth were too long (first day), [5] . [320.] ► In the evening, boring (tearing, grinding) in the teeth (after one hour), [1] . ► Boring in various hollow teeth, [1] . ► Drawing pain in the teeth of the right side, in the evening; it disappeared after having laid down, [3] . ► Gnawing pain in one of the front molar teeth of the lower jaw, with tearing behind the ear, as if the ear would be torn out; worse at nine o'clock in the evening, and then slowly decreasing; sitting up in the bed gives a little relief; after midnight there is only gnawing in the tooth; the pain remains the same under all circumstances; in daytime it is dull, [3] . ► Drawing and tearing pain in the front lower teeth, extending to the zygoma and the temples, [1] . ► Drawing pain, extending from one tooth as far as the ear, in the side of the head, [1] . ► Pressive pain in one of the incisors, both when chewing and not, [1] . ► Tearing in the molar teeth at different times of the day, sometimes as high up as the temples, [3] . ► On biting on a stump it pains, as if violently pushed into the socket, [1] . ► Cutting toothache, in the open air and on lying down in the evening bed (after two and three hours), [1] . [330.] ► Bruised pain in one of the right upper molar teeth; pressing upon the tooth, which makes it feel loose, relieves the pain (eleventh day), [3] . ► One of the upper molar teeth is painful to the touch, [1] . ► At night, when in bed, throbbing of the roots of the teeth, like pulsations, [6] . ► Jerking and tearing toothache wakes her up after midnight; it disappears after rising (fifth day), [3] . ► Jerking pain in one of the first molar teeth of the left upper row (first day), [3] . ► Tickling in the teeth and of their roots, immediately after dinner (the fourth and fifth days), [3] . ► Swelling of the gums , [1] . ► On the gums of the left side of the lower row of teeth an ulcer forms, which opens immediately, and discharges blood which tastes saltish, [3] . ► Bleeding of the gums (fourth day), [2] . ► Drawing pain in the gums, as if they were sore, [1] . [340.] ► Tongue coated white, with a good taste, [4] . ► Tongue coated yellowish-white, with bitter taste, [4] . ► Sense of roughness on the tongue (after three-quarters of an hour), [3] . ► Crawling in the tongue (stinging, and transient) (one hour), [5] . ► Itching in the tip of the tongue; he would like to scratch it to pieces (after fifth and seventh days), [3] . ► *Number of little ulcers in the mouth, [1] . ► Thick mucus runs from the posterior nares into the mouth, [1] . ► *Musty, bad smell from the mouth, [1] . ► The mouth continually feels as if it were burnt (after dinner) (after forty-eight hours), [3] . ► Painfulness of the inner mouth, palate, tongue, gums; they feel so sore that he can scarcely eat, [1] . [350.] ► On waking, the mouth is dry and the tongue sticks to the palate, [1] . ► Dryness in the mouth, although saliva is not wanting ; this causes frequent painful swallowing, [5] . ► *Increased secretion of saliva and mucus (first and second days), [3] , [5] . ► The secretion of saliva becomes real ptyalism, [5] . ► In the morning, spitting of much saliva and mucus, [6] . ► Increased secretion of saliva, with a sense of contraction in the mouth, or with continual crawling upon the surface of the cheeks (after half an hour), [5] . ► A large quantity of saliva accumulates in the mouth, when in bed in the evening (third day), [5] . ► Constant collection of sweetish or sourish water in the mouth (fifth and eighth days), [3] . ► Collection of much mucus in the mouth; on spitting it out it is continually formed, whilst the throat remains dry (first day), [5] . ► Water in the mouth, [4] . [360.] ► Sour liquid rises into his mouth, [1] . ► Frequent collection of watery saliva in the mouth; he is obliged to spit it out the whole day, most in the afternoon; never at night (after ten minutes, and second day), [3] . ► Early in the morning, flat, metallic taste in the mouth (fourth and fifth days), [5] . ► Sourish, saltish taste (first day), [3] . ► Bitter, insipid taste in the mouth, [2] . ► Bitter taste in the mouth (shortly after taking it), [3] . ► Bitter and slimy taste in the mouth, in the morning on rising (five days), [3] . ► Bitter taste in the evening, after having eaten apples, [1] . ► Astringent acrid taste upon the tongue, as from eating sloes (one to eight days), [3] , [5] . ► Taste in the mouth of blood, for half an hour (seven days), [3] . [370.] ► Everything tastes flat, [6] . ► Everything she eats seems tasteless, and not salted, especially in the evenings; bread tastes like sponge (first and second days), [3] . ► Meat, especially, does not seem to have any taste, [1] . ► Beer tastes bitter and nauseous; this causes retchings (twelve days), [3] .

throat.text: THROAT. ► *Redness, inflammatory, in the back part of the throat (nine days), [5] . ► Inflammation of the throat, terminated by a marked livid border round the buccal cavity, several days (after two days), [5] . ► *Thick viscid mucus flows into the throat from the posterior nares, [1] . ► He cannot hawk up the phlegm in his throat, because it is too far down, [3] . ► A piece of phlegm gets into his throat, which chokes him until he swallows it (after ten minutes), [3] . ► *Collection of thick viscid mucus in the throat, especially in the evening, and morning on waking , which increases the soreness of the throat; it induces frequent clearing of the throat, and can only be expectorated in little lumps, with great effort (first day), [5] . [380.] ► Clearing the throat of saltish phlegm, after dinner (two days), [3] . ►► *In the evening, dryness of the throat, which induces frequent clearing of the throat (three days), [5] . ► Dryness of the throat and mouth (shortly after taking it), [5] . ► *Dryness and scraping of the throat (one day), [3] . ► Great dryness of the throat, mouth, and lips, as if the parts were parched , attended with torturing thirst, [1] . ► Sourish taste in the throat, then bitter eructations, shortly after having eaten his milk soup in the evening (four days), [3] . ► Sourish taste appearing suddenly in his throat, without eructations, in the forenoon (four days), [3] . ► Sweet taste in the throat, with vertigo, followed by expectoration of mucus mixed with blood, early in the morning (twenty-eighth day), [3] . ► On waking in the morning, his throat is rough and his chest oppressed; he cannot raise anything from his throat, and is obliged to sneeze a good deal (after twelve hours), [1] . ► *In the evening (and at night), roughness of the throat, which causes-hawking, with secretion of mucus, several days in succession , [3] , [5] . [390.] ► Rancid and rough in the throat; this obliges him to clear the throat (four days), [3] . ► Hoarseness and rancidity of the throat, the whole afternoon (after five days), [3] . ► Sense of burning in the throat, in the evening (three days), [5] . ► Burning in the throat, like heartburn, with a sense of roughness (five days), [3] . ► In both sides of the neck, sensation as of an external swelling, with stinging pains, [1] . ► Sensation in the throat as if it were narrowed, and the air cut off, as in a violent sore throat after a cold, or in an inflammation of the throat; this always lasts only a few minutes (first seven days), [2] . ► Constrictive (pressive) pain in the throat and pharynx, with much mucus in the mouth, in the evening (one, two days), [5] . ► *Scraping high up in the throat , [1] . ► Drawing and tensive pain in the right side of the throat, especially on moving the tongue (nine days), [5] . ► At nights, spasmodically drawing pain in one side of the throat and in the ear, disturbing the sleep, and increased by deglutition (nine days), [5] . [400.] ► *Pressure in the throat as from a lump, with soreness, rough voice, and dryness of the throat , [1] . ► Pressive and tensive pain in the inner right side of the throat, as far as the ear (one day), [5] . ► Sense of scraping in the throat, as if he had swallowed pepper (after three hours), [5] . ► Constant griping scraping in the throat (five days), [3] . ► After eructation, scraping in the throat, which obliges clearing the throat (two days), [3] . ► *Rawness and roughness of the throat , which induce coughing, in the evening (four days), [3] , [5] . ► Sore throat in swallowing , [4] . ► Burning soreness in the throat during and after deglutition, in the morning, for several days in succession (four days), [5] . ► *After clearing the throat, which she finds very difficult, it feels very sore , [3] . ► Aching sore throat during empty deglutition, in the evening, for several days in succession (caused by a swelling in the throat) (four days), [5] . [410.] ► Sore throat, pressure in the throat, even when not swallowing, with internal heat in both hands (after two hours), [1] . ► *Transient stitches in the throat, shooting here and there, on swallowing, and something pointed sometimes seems to stick in the throat in the evening (two, four days), [5] . ► Stinging in the throat, on empty deglutition, [2] , [3] . ► *Violent tickling in the throat, exciting frequent cough (four days), [5] . ► Pressive pain in the left tonsil, during and after swallowing (one day), [5] . ► In the morning, dull stitches in the right tonsil (four days), [5] . ► Swollen tonsils (six days), [5] . ► *Sense as of constriction from the sophagus down to the stomach, every time he swallows a morsel of food , [1] . ► Contraction of the sophagus, and want of action, in the morning, on waking, [1] . ► *Violent pressive pain, as if a portion of the sophagus were contracted or compressed , in the middle of the chest, especially during deglutition, but also when not swallowing, with oppression of the chest alternating with palpitation of the heart, especially after a meal (eight, nine days), [5] . [420.] ► Impeded deglutition at night, as from a spasmodic constriction of the throat (one day), [5] . ► Spasmodically pressive pain in the middle of the chest, on swallowing food and drink, [5] .

stomach.text: STOMACH. ► He is always hungry; could always be eating, [1] . ► Very hungry, [2] . ► Rapid hunger; he is panting for his dinner, and can scarcely await it, [4] . ► She is hungry, but she eats nothing with relish, [3] . ► She has no aversion to food, but no desire to eat; on looking at the food she is already satisfied; she could go a whole day without eating (for many days), [1] . ► Strong appetite for vegetables, fruit, and soft food, [1] . ► *No desire to eat, no appetite , no hunger; food does not taste badly, but it does not taste at all; everything tastes like straw or shavings, [1] . ► He has no appetite , and eats with reluctance, [4] . [430.] ► Little hunger and no appetite for many days, even when he eats nothing at dinner (one to fifteen days), [3] . ► Diminished appetite, with fulness of the abdomen, [5] . ► *Aversion to meat , [1] . ► Aversion to meat, even unto vomiting, for three days (after six days), [3] . ► Very thirsty the whole day, also at dinner, [3] . ► Aversion to the usual smoking, [1] . ► Smoking does not relish, and intoxicates him, for four days, [3] . ► Smoking does not agree with him, [1] . ► Frequent empty eructations (after two hours), [1] . ► Empty eructations after supper (two days), [3] . [440.] ► Frequent eructations tasting of milk-soup which he had taken, from supper until he lay down, [3] . ► Eructations, with pressive pain in the chest, while eating (after three-quarters of an hour), [3] . ► Bitter eructations after eating potatoes; he feels a loathing which makes him shiver; in the evening (five days), [2] . ► Rancid eructations, leaving a burning in the throat for a long while (one day), [5] . ► Rancid eructations, especially after dinner (ten to thirteen days), [5] . ► Rancid eructations after breakfast soup, [3] . ► Acrid eructations, [1] . ► Sour eructations , in the evening, when in bed, [1] . ► *Sour eructations , with burning in the throat, like heartburn, (one day), [3] . ► In the forenoon, acidity rises into his mouth; it continues for a long time, and is attended with a sensation of heat in the mouth, [1] . [450.] ► Waterbrash, [4] . ► Gulping up of sour mucus; afterwards, burning in the throat, like heartburn, coming on frequently, especially after the soup for breakfast, [1] . ► Sweetish risings from the stomach; the phlegm which he throws up tastes sweetish, continues a long time, early in the morning (three days), [3] . ► Hiccough after eating, at noon and in the evening, [1] . ► Hiccough after dinner, also after eructations from the breakfast soup (one to two days), [3] . ► When standing, she becomes nauseated, [1] . ► Nausea, even to fainting; her breathing is arrested on account of it at night, [1] . ► Nausea as soon as 4 o'clock in the morning, [1] . ► Frequent nausea, as if he would vomit; his appetite is, nevertheless, tolerably good, [1] . ► Inclination to vomit in the morning, [4] . [460.] ► In the morning, retching in the throat, as if one would vomit, [1] . ► Inclination to vomit after eructation of air, with chilliness of the body, which rises from the feet into the abdomen, [4] . ► Nausea, with an inclination to vomit, and retching; this caused her to put her finger into the throat, after which she vomited phlegm and water, but the breakfast, which she had eaten two hours before, remained (fourteen days), [4] . ► After supper, nausea, loathing, and weariness, for several evenings, [1] . ► Loathing and qualmishness in the sophagus (one hour), [1] . ► Qualmishness at the stomach, with faint-like nausea and vertigo; the room turns round with her; after this, her head remains confused (ten days), [4] . ► Frequent nausea, especially during the chills, [6] . ► Nausea, with rising of air, [4] . ► Nausea and chilliness the whole day, [4] . ► Attack of nausea, with headache, paleness of the face, want of appetite, several evacuations of the bowels, loathing, subsequent inclination to vomit, creeping chilliness; after a walk he is obliged to lie down (eleven days), [2] . [470.] ► Early in the morning, faint-like nausea; after breakfast, relieved (nine days), [4] . ► On waking in the morning, nausea, qualmishness, and lassitude, with stickings over the eyes, and pain in the kidneys during motion (nine days), [4] . ► She wakes in the morning with nausea and qualmishness at the stomach, and physical depression, as if sleep had not recruited her, accompanied by a quick feverish pulse, with internal heat (three days), [4] . ► Violent nausea and tremor after supper, [1] . ► Disagreeable sensation of hunger and emptiness in the stomach, without, however, much appetite, [1] . ► Violent pain in the stomach, with sensitiveness to external pressure in the region of the stomach in the evening (six days), [3] . ► Pain at the stomach; it feels full or bloated; painful to external touch; empty eructations and fermentations, or loud rumbling and rollings in the abdomen, as of something working therein, after dinner (one to five days), [3] . ► After having eaten potatoes, the stomach aches; sick feeling, nausea, and then colic, [3] . ► Sensation of coldness in the stomach, as if she had been drinking cold water, in the evening; also in the forenoon, after an eructation, and while some eructations are continually taking place; in the afternoon; it passes off (two to five days), [3] . ► Heartburn after drinking water, [1] . [480.] ► Heartburn after supper, [1] . ► Heartburn , with profuse discharge of water from the mouth, [2] . ► Sensation of pinching at the stomach, in the afternoon (one day), [3] . ► Drawing pain at the stomach, [1] . ► After dinner, drawing in the stomach; this caused a sensation of tension in the whole body, which made her so tired that she was obliged to lie down, [1] . ► Gnawing in the region of the stomach, awhile after dinner (two to three days), [5] . ► Pressure and constriction in the region of the stomach (thirty-one days), [2] . ► Pressure and soreness transversely across the stomach and upper abdomen, in the afternoon, [1] . ► Disagreeable pressure in the region of the stomach before the evacuations had taken place (nine days), [2] . ► Oppression at the stomach upon eating, although she eats with relish, [4] . [490.] ► Oppression at the stomach, extending up to the throat, after having eaten potatoes; eructations relieves, in the morning (eight days), [3] . ► Oppression in the stomach, towards noon or evening, [2] , [3] . ► Griping in the pit of the stomach, in the evening, when in bed, [1] . ► Drawing pain from the pit of the stomach, up into the pharynx, with difficult breathing, [1] . ► Violent pressure at the pit of the stomach, and from thence great oppression of the chest; she was obliged to stand still every moment, and was unable to walk further, [4] . ► Sensation of pressure and constriction at the pit of the stomach, extending into the chest and between the scapulæ (thirteen days), [2] . ► Pressure and crawling at the pit, as from a worm (after two hours), [3] . ► Twisting and constriction in the region of the stomach, extending into the chest and throat, attended with difficult breathing, [2] , [3] . ► Sensation of cutting in the region of the stomach; it is painful to the touch, in the afternoon (two days), [3] . ► Stitches in the pit of the stomach and of the chest, [1] . [500.] ► After dinner, stitches in the stomach and chest, coming out at the shoulder, with short breathing and great anguish; for several hours in the evening (after twelve days), [3] . ► After an evacuation, and during the period of digestion, he experiences a sense of scraping in the stomach and the mouth, [1] . ► Ulcerative soreness at the pit of the stomach, early in the morning, on turning in the bed, [1] . ► Tearing sore pain from the pit of stomach down into the abdomen, as if everything would be torn out, [1] . >>>>> Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Alum.

rectum.text: STOOL AND ANUS. ► Protrusion of a varix of the rectum; it enlarges by walking, and decreases during a night's rest, [1] . ► Varices of the rectum always enlarge in the evening; they become moist, and burn, [1] . ► The rectum is inactive, as if it were deficient in peristaltic motion, and had not strength enough to press the contents out ; the stools are soft and thin, and can only be pressed out by straining the abdominal muscles (after sixteen hours), [1] . ►► The rectum seems paralyzed (second day), [1] . [590.] ► The varices become moist, and sting, [1] . ► When walking, dark-colored blood passes out of her rectum, [1] . ► Tenesmus of the rectum and the bladder, passing off after an evacuation, [4] . ► Tenesmus (third day), [1] . ► Tenesmus (first day), [3] . ► Tenesmus, lasting a long while, succeeding a troublesome pressure in the lower abdomen; the evacuation comes on slowly, and can only be passed by straining the abdominal muscles; the whole of the intestines appear to be inactive for want of peristaltic motion; stools are not hard (after second day), [1] . ► Tenesmus preceding an evacuation, [1] . ► Bearing down during an evacuation; the stools are very firm, knotty, and scanty, preceded by tenesmus (second day), [3] . ► *Itching, burning of the anus , [1] . ► During an evacuation it seemed as if the rectum were dried up and constricted ; however, the stool was regular, [4] . [600.] ► Pricking, as with pins, in the anus, after a difficult evacuation, [1] . ► Crawling in rectum, as from worms, [4] . ► Itching, burning, and stinging of the rectum, [4] . ► *Sensation of excoriation in the anus, after an evacuation attended with contraction of the rectum and constriction of the anus , [1] . ► Pressure in the anus (third day), [5] . ► Itching in the anus for a long while (after thirtieth day), [2] . ► Itching of the anus, as if it would pulsate, [4] . ► Severe itching in the fold between the nates and the anus; scratching aggravates it (first and second days), [3] . ► Sweat of the perineum, with an intolerable itching, which increases, and becomes painful after rubbing, [2] . ► Sensation as if the perineum, between the scrotum and anus, were inflamed, [4] . [610.] ► Momentary pressure in the perineum on blowing the nose, [4] . ► Painful pressure in the perineum, which passes off quickly, [4] . ► Violent pressure in the perineum, at the commencement of coition and during erections, [4] . ► Pressure in the perineum during coition, [4] . ► Soreness of the perineum during clap (after four weeks), [2] . ► Colic preceding an evacuation, which is sometimes solid, sometimes soft, but always scanty, [4] . ► After an evacuation, much ineffectual desire for stool in the upper abdomen, and in the sides of the abdomen, without tenesmus, [1] . ► Attacks of slight diarrhic stools, with colic, lasting for two and three days, [1] . ► Diarrha succeeding colic, [4] . ► Diarrha succeeding a retention of stool, which had lasted six days; six evacuations a day, preceded by colic, which sometimes continued even after the evacuation, [4] . [620.] ► Twice a diarrha in the evening; the stools at last become lumpy (second day), [3] . ► Diarrha, with tenesmus in rectum , [4] . ► For some time she had three or four ordinary evacuations a day without any trouble, [2] . ► The evacuation which generally took place in the evening, took place in the morning, [4] . ► Liquid stools, preceded by pain in the abdomen, or attended with colic (third and fifth day), [3] . ► Stools soft, almost liquid, with burning in the anus, also an evacuation in the evening, preceded by tenesmus; another in the night (second and fifth days), [3] . ► The first portion of her stools is liquid, and is expelled with a jerk; the last portion appears burned (fifth day), [3] . ► Stools covered all over with a whitish mucus, firm, preceded by oppression in the region of the stomach, which ceased immediately after the evacuation (after thirtieth day), [2] . ► Bright-colored stools, [1] . ► Bloody mucus passes during and between the evacuations and without it, [1] . [630.] ► *Dropping of blood during an evacuation , [1] . ► *This is succeeded by a stream of flood, followed by soreness in the anus, and along the rectum (after seventeenth day), [1] . ► *Blood passes during a firm evacuation (ninth and thirtieth days), [2] . ► Retention of stool (eighth and eleventh days), [3] . ► Stool only once every two days, solid, sometimes mixed with blood, [4] . ► No stool during the first days, [4] . ► *Stools too scanty , [2] . ►► *Evacuation of a small quantity of hard fæces, with pressure and a sensation of excoriation in the rectum , [1] . ► Hard and difficult stools, with pain in the anus, [2] . ► *Difficult evacuations, the stools being hard and of the shape of laurel berries, with cutting pain in the anus, as if it were too narrow, [1] . [640.] ► *Firm, hard, scanty stools, with pressure and pain in the anus, and difficult evacuation (the first day), [3] .

urinary.text: URINARY ORGANS. ► *Feeling of weakness in the bladder and genital organs ; he fears that he will wet the bed (in the evening), [6] . ► Violent tenesmus of the bladder, [3] , [6] . ► Pressure and drawing in the region of the bladder, especially at the neck of the bladder (fourth and fifth days), [5] . ► (Clap for more than six weeks (after fourteenth day), with a violent and painful swelling of the inguinal glands of the right side, cutting pain during micturition, and pain in the perineum, especially violent at the end of the second week; the pain in the perineum is especially violent when standing, rising, or sitting down), [2] . ► Sensation of heat in the urethra, which passes off by lying still, [4] . ► (Feeling of dryness in the anterior portion of the urethra, as if the skin there were without sensibility, especially in the morning), [4] . ► Tearings and shootings in the urethra when walking in the open air, ascending towards the hypogastrium, [1] . ► Agreeable and voluptuous itching in the urethra and between the testicles, [4] . ► Itching, burning in the urethra, [1] . [650.] ► Early in the morning, on waking, while in bed, a drawing and biting sensation in the urethra, [1] . ► Cutting in the anterior part of the urethra during micturition, and even awhile afterwards, as if the urine passed over an inflamed place (after eighteenth day), [2] . ► After urinating the urethra became hot, then burning, and he had tenesmus of both rectum and bladder, [4] . ► Urging and pressure to urinate, without increased quantity of urine, [5] . ► Urging to urinate early in the morning, on waking, with difficult and tardy emission of the urine in a thin stream, from the female urethra (seventh day), [1] . ► (When he wishes to urinate he has a sort of fright), [4] . ► When urinating, burning like fire; much worse in the evening (first day), [3] , [4] . ► He is frequently obliged to rise during the night to urinate (first and fourth days), [3] . ► (After sitting a long time he feels no inconvenience in urinating, but as soon as he moves about he feels a burning), [4] . ► (Urinating is followed by burning, which makes him low-spirited and desponding), [4] . [660.] ► During her menses she was obliged to urinate frequently day and night, which corroded the genital organs (sixth day), [4] . ► Frequent emission of urine in small quantity in the evening (first day), [3] . ► Increased secretion of urine for several days, [3] , [5] . ► Involuntary emissions of urine, occasioned by bearing down in order to evacuate the bowels, which can only be effected with great difficulty (second day), [1] . ► (Involuntary emission of urine, at least twenty times a day; very little urine passes off at a time); (with a clap) (after four weeks), [2] . ► Seldom, but copious emission of urine (sixth day), [3] . ► Increased secretion of pale, hot urine, with burning, [3] , [5] . ► Emission of much straw-yellow, clear urine (fourth and fifth days), [3] , [5] . ► Large quantity of water-colored urine, [1] . ► Frequent and copious emission of pale urine (preceded by burning in the urethra), [3] . [670.] ► (Twisted stream of urine), [4] . ► Decrease of urine (in the morning), with cutting in the anterior part of the urethra (fourth, fifth, and sixth days), [3] . ► For a whole day there is no evacuation, either of urine or fæces, [3] . ► No emission of urine in the forenoon, but, in the afternoon, frequent emissions of an increased quantity of a reddish urine, which becomes turbid during the night, and deposits a sediment (first day), [3] . ► She passes but little urine, which deposits a sediment or red sand, [1] . ► The deep yellow urine soon deposits a large and loose cloud (first and fifth days), [3] . ► When standing, the urine deposits a thick white sediment, [2] . ► Pale urine, with a turbid sediment, [3] . ► White, turbid urine, as if chalk had been stirred with it, [4] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Numerous erections in the evening, and during the night when lying in bed, and in the afternoon when sitting (first to third day), [5] . [680.] ► (When waking, during the night, almost constantly painful erections; they are not perfect, but cause a sensation as if the organ were ulcerated internally, with short, fine, piercing stitches in the whole organ, resembling shootings) (after four weeks), [2] . ► (Priapisms during the night), [4] . ► On moving his hand along the penis, has a drawing pain, which extends as far as the glans; with weak appetite, [4] . ► Soreness on the inner surface of the prepuce, [1] . ► Secretion of much smegma behind the glans, [1] . ► Sensation as if the glans were compressed (for two minutes), [4] . ► Drawing from the glans through the urethra (after five days), [5] . ► Formation upon the glans, [4] . ► Itching of the glans (fourth day), [5] . ► Itching of the scrotum; removed by scratching (second day), [3] . [690.] ► Contractive pain in the right spermatic cord; the testicle of the right side is drawn up at the same time, and is also very sore and painful (second day), [5] . ► The left testicle is hard, and indescribably painful to the touch, [4] . ► At first, the sexual instinct appears to be lessened, and the erections appear to be increased; whereas, in the reaction of the organism, the desire for an embrace increases, although erection is wanting, [1] . ► Indifference to sexual inter-course, [4] . ► Want of sexual instinct (immediately, for several days), [1] . ► During the first weeks, the sexual instinct is more intense, in the following weeks decreasing, and more moderate, [1] . ► Frequent and violent erections and emissions (after three and thirty-three days), [2] , [5] . ► Emissions, two nights in succession (after fifteen days), [1] . ► Emission during the afternoon nap, [1] . ► Almost every other night an emission, with voluptuous dreams, [1] . [700.] ► Emissions, the first four nights, with voluptuous dreams, [1] . ► During coition the semen passes thick and lumpy like jelly, [4] . ► Pressure in the sexual organs, [1] . ► Tickling of the sexual organs and the thighs, [1] . ► Jerking stitches on the left side of the pudendum, extending into the chest, [1] . ► Itching in the pudenda, during the leucorrha, [3] . ► Pain, like the tick of a watch, on the left side of the vagina, with throbbing as in formation of pus in an ulcer, which continues for two days, unchanged under all conditions; however, nothing could be either seen or felt (thirty-sixth day), [4] . ► Leucorrha, [3] . ► (The leucorrha with which she was affected, ceased), [2] . ► Leucorrha succeeding the menses, painless, lasting three days (after twenty-seventh day), [4] . [710.] ► Frequently acrid leucorrha , [1] . ► *Acrid leucorrha, with burning in the genital organs, and still more in the rectum; these parts seemed inflamed and corroded, so that she found walking difficult; washing with cold water relieved; the leucorrha was abundant, and flowed almost over her feet ; at the same time blood, resembling meat washings, passed for two days; three days after the menses (twenty-second day), [4] . ► Leucorrha, looking like meat washings, in the afternoon, when walking in the open air (and when sitting), also at night, [3] . ► *Profuse leucorrha of transparent mucus, only in daytime, without sensation or pain in the abdomen, [1] . ► *Leucorrha, quite clear, like water or transparent mucus; it made the linen stiff (after eight days), [4] . ► *Leucorrha of yellow mucus (after some days), [1] . ► Six days before the appearance of the menses, she has a copious flow of mucus from the vagina, accompanied by tremor, lassitude, and a sensation as if everything would fall out of her abdomen, [1] . ► The menses appear four days too soon, preceded by violent headache, which ceased on the appearance of the menses; after they had flowed for one day, the headache came on again, and continued during the whole period, for five days; the menses were less copious than usual (after twenty-two days), [4] . ► The period came on five days too early; a copious flow on the second day; lasted eight days, as usual; preceded by pains in the abdomen; diarrha on the sixth day, [4] . ► Menses appear too soon (by three to eleven days), short and scanty, [3] . [720.] ► The menses, which had gradually stopped, appear again (after seventeen days), [1] . ► The period had already delayed ten days (after fifty-two days); during a walk and a desire for urinating, a little dark-colored lymph passed; the menses did not appear till the third month (in a woman of forty-eight years), [1] . ► Appearance of scanty menses (after ninth day); four weeks later (after thirty-seven days), they flow copiously, [1] . ► The menses are scanty, and last only three days, [3] . ► Menses scanty and pale (after three days), [1] . ► The menses appear on the sixth day, without any disagreeable symptoms; two days later, a cold comes on with pain in the nose, the head, and the forehead, increased on blowing the nose; in the later days; diarrha and colic intervene besides, [4] .
Muscles of bladder paretic, must strain at stool in order to urinate. Pain in kidneys, with mental confusion. Frequent desire to urinate in old people. Difficult starting

Pain in the kidneys, principally when walking or stooping. Sensation of weakness in the bladder and in the genital parts. Eager desire to make water, with increased aqueous evacuation, accompanied sometimes by a sensation of burning. Frequent ineffectual desire to urinate: the urine can only be passed during a stool. Urine less copious, with red and sandy sediment. Nocturnal urination. Urine turbid, white, as if chalk had been put into it. Thick whitish sediment in the urine.

Renal pains as from riding over a rough road ; alternating with cloudiness as if drunk ; dancing makes worse. Frequent micturition. θ Prolapsus uteri. Tenesmus vesicæ. θ Dysentery. Urine voided while straining at stool ; or cannot pass urine without such straining. Frequent urination at night. Frequent emissions of very small quantities of lightcolored urine with great straining, severe smarting and burning, with a feeling as though a few drops remained in urethra which could not be expelled. Urine : scanty, with red sediment, in arthritic affections ; copious and pale, in nervous diseases ; with thick, white sediment ; more frequent, copious and dark. Urine red without sediment ; met. θ Spinal disease. Feeling of weakness in bladder and genitals, in evening, with fear that he will wet the bed. Pouting of meatus urinarius, with swelling and discharge of light yellow pus from urethra. During urination : burning, with discharge of urine and desire to evacuate bowels. Urine smarting, corroding. θ Prolapsus uteri.

respiratory.text: RESPIRATORY APPARATUS. ► Phlegm in the air-passages, always adherent; it necessitates clearing the throat, whereby only a small portion becomes detached, [2] . ► Whistling in the air-passages, and smothering in the chest, on breathing, [1] . ► *Irritation in the larynx induces cough, [4] . ► Irritation to cough, with frequent spitting of saliva, [5] . [730.] ► She takes cold easily , in the room; she gets hoarse, this decreases by walking in the open air, [1] . ► Hoarseness in the morning (sixteenth day), [1] . ► She is frequently attacked with sudden hoarseness, which caused a loss of voice, and could not be relieved by clearing the throat; mostly in the afternoon and evening, [1] . ► Cough, with painful pressure in the occiput, [1] . ► Cough, with scraping in the throat, [3] . ► Short fits of cough, with tearing, shooting pain in the right temple and the vertex, [1] . ► Cough, which makes the chest feel sore, in the forenoon (third day), [3] . ► Frequent short, dry cough, in the forenoon and evening, [3] , [5] . ► *Dry cough at night, with dryness of the throat (after twenty-four hours), [3] . ► Violent, short, dry persistent cough, with sneezing; and tearing, shooting, and pinching pain, extending from the nape of the neck as far as the right shoulder, [1] . [740.] ► Dry cough, coming suddenly in the morning, and passing off quickly, or when walking in the open air, and then also continuing in the room (first and sixth day), [3] . ►► *Continual dry, hacking cough, with vomiting and arrest of breathing , and sticking pain extending from the left side of the abdomen to the hypochondrium and the pit of the stomach, [1] . ► Violent dry cough, during the day, every attack lasts a long time; only after two days, the cough becomes less frequent and looser, [1] . ► Violent and dry cough in the morning after rising; later, some expectoration follows (fourth and sixth days), [3] . ► *Cough, with much expectoration, especially in the morning, [1] . ► (Cough, with expectoration; the throat becomes rough and hoarse, with fluent coryza), [4] . ► Cough, with secretion of loose mucus (curative effect), [4] . ► Sudden violent, but short cough; by making an effort, he expectorates a piece of phlegm, mixed with blood, in the afternoon (fifth day), [3] . ► The morning cough and expectoration ceased (curative effects), (fifth day), [4] . ► Difficult breathing, in the forenoon, [4] . >>>>> Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Alum.

Cough soon after waking in the morning. Hoarse, aphonia, tickling in larynx; wheezing, rattling respiration. Cough on talking or singing, in the morning. Chest feels constricted. Condiments produce cough. Talking aggravates soreness of chest

VOICE AND LARYNX. TRACHEA AND BRONCHIA. [25] [Alum.]. Voice : has a nasal twang ; husky ; rough. Hoarseness, evening and night, especially towards morning ; disappearing while walking in open air. Sudden, complete aphonia. Hoarseness. Hoarse afternoons and evenings, with roughness and dryness. Voice hoarse, rough, hollow, almost aphonic ; met. θ Spinal disease. Much talking, particularly in lively company, makes her hoarse. Talking or singing makes him cough. Talking increases soreness of chest. Rawness in larynx on awaking. Tickling in larynx, with irritation to cough. Sensation of tightly adhering phlegm in larynx, not removed by hawking or cough ; wheezing on inspiration. Thyroid cartilage painful to touch. Throat dry and voice husky on waking. Rattling, asthmatic breathing ; < coughing. Oppression < when sitting stooped ; > straightening up, or walking in open air. Talking or singing makes him cough. Breathing arrested by copious, thick, tenacious, saltish mucus. Oppressive pain in chest, tight feeling. Dyspnœa, with spasmodic pains in stomach and hypochondria. Short-breathed when lying on back ; goes off by turning on side. Worse getting tired from exerting voice. Worse from deep inspiration ; shooting from left hypochondria to heart. Wheezing inspiration, with tough mucus in larynx. Dry, hacking cough, with frequent sneezing. Cough : from sensation as of loose skin hanging in throat ; from tickling in larynx ; from elongated uvula ; from talking or singing ; short ; causes pains in right temple and top of head, sometimes also difficult breathing ; soon after waking in morning ; every morning a long attack of dry cough, ending in difficult raising of a little white mucus ; with tearing pain and involuntary emission of urine, in old or withered looking people ; sudden, violent irrepressible cough in evening while sitting ; sometimes yields a piece of mucus mixed with blood ; causes shooting in vertex. All irritating things excite cough. Painless evening cough. Dry cough at night, with dryness of throat. Continued dry hacking cough, with vomiting and arrest of breathing. Sputum difficult and of a putrid taste.

heart.text: HEART AND PULSE. ► Palpitation of the heart, daily on waking, [1] . [800.] ► *Frequent palpitation of the heart; a few irregular beats, large and small ones being intermixed, [1] . ► Towards 4 or 5 o'clock in the morning, waked by anxiety about the heart, as if sweat would break out, which, however, is not the case; after rising, the anxiety immediately disappears, [1] .

back.text: NECK AND BACK. ► The nape of the neck is painful on moving the head, [1] . ► Stitches in the nape of the neck, [1] . ► Stitches in the nape of the neck and on the right side of the neck, where they disappear only after long friction, [3] . ► Itching in the nape of the neck and of the throat (after eighteen days), [3] , [4] . ► Vesicles on the right side of the neck (night days), [3] . ► Swelling of the glands of the left side of the neck, [1] . ► Early in the morning, stiffness of the neck and upper part of the back, with drawing pains, passing off by motion (four days), [5] . ► Stiffness of the muscles of the neck, so that she cannot turn her head to the left side, [1] . [810.] ► Violent tension in the back of the neck, one hour in the afternoon (one day), [3] . ► Painful drawing in the muscles of the neck, not altered by friction or motion of the head, in the forenoon (one day), [3] . ► Drawing and pressure in the glands of the left side of the neck, [1] . ► Stitches in the neck, on the left side, externally, relieved by pressure, with tearing in the head and stitches in the ears (after twelve days), [4] . ► Stitches in the glands of the right side of the neck, and drawing pressure in those of the left side (after half an hour to an hour), [5] . ► Violent itching of the neck and on the chest, as if she were bitten by fleas (after ten days), [4] . ► Violent itching of the neck, nape of the neck, and of the chest, without any visible eruption; on touching the skin, a hard pimple is felt here and there under the skin (fourteen days), [4] . ► Painful stiffness between the scapulæ, afterwards extending towards the ribs and kidneys, [6] . ► Burning in the superior angle of the left scapula, relieved somewhat by friction, [3] . ► Painful tension between the scapulæ, in the forenoon (two days), [3] . [820.] ► Gnawing stitches in the scapulæ (second day), [3] . ► Stitches between the scapulæ, [1] . ► Stitches between (and on) the scapulæ, with arrest of breathing (first, second, third days), [3] . ► Tearing pain in the left scapula (after thirty-four days), [1] . ► Stitches and cutting in the scapulæ, with chilliness, two days in succession, [1] . ► Itching on and between the scapulæ, [3] . ► *Pain in the back, as if a hot iron were thrust through the lower vertebræ , [1] . ► Violent pressure in the back, preceding the protrusion of a varix from the rectum, [1] . ► Violent pain all along the back; stitches and twitches so that she cannot stoop, and cannot take up anything with her hand; increased by breathing (third day), [2] . ► Fine stitches, extending from the back to the ribs (after two hours), [3] . [830.] ► *From time to time, a violent stitch in the middle of the back, [1] . ► Itching, crawling (and stinging) in the whole of the back and in the sacrum, succeeded by grumbling pain, [3] . ► Pain in the region of the kidneys, [1] . ► Pain in the region of both kidneys, across the small of the back, as from a bruise, or after driving upon a rough road; it is worse on stooping or turning; as if a hook were plunged into her, which makes her scream, and lasts for a few days (fourth day), [4] . ► In the morning, pain in the kidneys; better in the afternoon, [4] . ► Pain in the loins, especially on walking or stooping (tenth day), [4] . ► Burning itching, like a fleabite, in the left lumbar region; it makes him start; lasts a long time, and did not go off till after long scratching, [3] . ► Pain in the small of the back when walking, [1] . ► Violent pain in the small of the back, like gnawing; it rises up to between the shoulders, where it becomes so violent that she would like to weep (relieved by Chamomile) (thirty-second day), [3] . ► Tearing-sticking pain in the small of the back, in the evening before falling asleep, in bed, [1] . [840.] ► Tearing jerkings in the small of the back, especially during motion, [1] . ► Pain, as if bruised, in the small of the back and in the back , [1] . ► Violent pain, as from a bruise, in the small of the back, and (early in the morning) in the coccyx on touching the part (fourth and seventh day), [3] . ► After the evacuation, throbbing in the small of the back, [1] . ► Painful twitches at the point of the coccyx, in the forenoon (second day), [3] . ► Gnawing pain in the coccyx, not altered by walking; relieved by stretching (first day, in the evening), [3] . ► Itching in the os coccygis, [1] .
Stitches. Gnawing pain, as if from hot iron. Pain along cord, with paralytic weakness

Itching of nape of neck and throat. Stitches in nape. Pain in the loins during repose. Pain like that of a bruise in the loins and in the back. Shooting pains in the back. Sensation in the back as if it were pierced with a hot iron; through the lower vertebræ.

Itching in nape of neck and of throat. Shooting in right side of neck, posterior portion. Pain in back and small of back, as if beaten. Pain in back, as if a hot iron was thrust through lower vertebræ. At intervals, sudden sharp pains like a stab in lower part of back, which made her feel for the moment as if she could not straighten spine. Cervical muscles drew head back during sleep ; met. θ Spinal disease. Violent stitch in middle of back. Gnawing pain in back. Throbbing in small of back during stool. Pain in small of back during rest. Pain in sacrum. θ Uterine troubles.

extremities.text: EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► Twitchings in all the limbs, [1] . ► Involuntary twitchings here and there; movements of one foot, of the fingers, etc., [1] . ► In the evening, twitchings in both of the lower extremities together, especially in the legs and feet, followed by a disposition to turn the arms and stretch them upwards, [1] . [850.] ► In the morning, paralytic weakness of all the limbs, with stupefaction of the head, in paroxysms of some minutes (tenth day), [1] . ► The fourth and fifth fingers, the right knee, and at last the heel, go to sleep after sitting, [1] . ► Rigidity of the hands and feet, as if they had gone to sleep, early, on waking; the symptom disappeared after rising and walking a little, [1] . ► When engaged in some bodily labor, a cramplike sensation, like numbness, ascending the whole of the left leg, also in the left arm, with dizzy stupefaction of the head, in paroxysms (fourteenth day), [1] . ► Sensation as of burning tension in the dorsa of the feet and hands, as from a swelling, [1] . ► Sense of contraction in the right index and foot, as if the tendons were too short; when he touches something with his finger, he feels as if he were being electrified, [4] . ► *Pain in the limbs as if the bones were squeezed narrower, with pressure in the joints, [1] . ► Sensation in the limbs as if he had caught cold, accompanied by frequent chilliness during the day and heat in the face in the evening, [1] . ► Tearing in the left scapula, in the arms, hands, and legs, especially in the evening (third day), [1] . UPPER EXTREMITIES. ► Tearing, at different periods, in the arms and all the parts thereof, in the shoulders, axillæ, upper arms, elbows, forearms, etc., [3] .
